name: Vuelta de Collserola desde la estación Valldoreix
region_intro: Mountain biking around Sant Cugat Del Vallès is characterized by its location within the Parc Natural de la Serra de Collserola, offering a diverse landscape of green, mountainous terrain. The region features extensive woodlands, hills, and a network of paths suitable for various outdoor pursuits. This proximity to a large metropolitan park provides numerous opportunities for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Sant Cugat Del Vallès The most popular mountain bike trail is Vuelta de Collserola desde la estación Valldoreix, a 42.4 miles (68.3 km) difficult trail that takes about 6 hours to complete. This route offers a challenging ride through varied terrain within the Collserola Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vuelta de Sabadell por Camí del Monjos y Cami de la Font de la Mina, a moderate 31.0 miles (49.9 km) path. This route features a mix of dirt and gravel roads, providing a balanced riding experience. Local mountain bikers also love the Sant Medir and La Salut Hermitages Loop, a 19.7 miles (31.7 km) trail leading through woodlands and past historical hermitages, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Mountain biking around Sant Cugat Del Vallès is defined by the extensive woodlands of Collserola Natural Park, varied hilly terrain, and numerous dirt and gravel paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Sant Cugat Del Vallès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 4000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sant Cugat Del Vallès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: Sant Cugat Del Vallès offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 60 routes available. These trails are primarily located within the extensive Parc Natural de la Serra de Collserola, providing a diverse landscape for riders.

Q: What kind of mountain bike trail difficulties can I expect in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: The trails around Sant Cugat Del Vallès cater to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, including 7 easy routes perfect for beginners or a relaxed ride, 45 moderate trails for those seeking a balanced challenge, and 11 difficult routes for experienced mountain bikers looking for more technical terrain and elevation.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: Yes, there are family-friendly options available. The region features 7 easy mountain bike trails, many of which are within the Parc Natural de la Serra de Collserola. Areas like Pla de Reixac offer expansive, gentle slopes and easy paths, ideal for families or those looking to practice basic MTB techniques in a relaxed setting.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e933455634/sant-medir-and-la-salut-hermitages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Medir and La Salut Hermitages Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 31.7 km circular trail that takes you through woodlands and past historical sites. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926728261/sant-cugat-sant-quirze-cami-del-monjos-rubi-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Cugat, Sant Quirze, Camí del Monjos, Rubí - circular&lt;/a&gt; route, offering a 32.4 km moderate ride.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass through the beautiful Serra de Collserola Natural Park, offering extensive woodlands and diverse ecosystems. You might encounter historical sites like the 12th-century Torre Negra. For specific natural attractions, consider routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Borrell Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient Pi d'en Xandri pine tree, a natural monument within the Parc de la Pollancreda.

Q: Are there any gorges or lakes accessible from the mountain bike trails?
A: While mountain biking, you can find routes that lead to or pass near several natural water features. The region has highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1423555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de la Mola&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2956234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Escletxes del Papiol&lt;/a&gt;, which are gorges. For lakes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4482632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallvidrera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Borrell Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; are notable points of interest that can be incorporated into or viewed from various routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: Sant Cugat Del Vallès, with its temperate Mediterranean climate, generally offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with milder temperatures and lush scenery. Even in summer, the extensive woodlands of Collserola Natural Park provide welcome shade, though it's advisable to ride earlier in the morning or later in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: The mountain bike trails in Sant Cugat Del Vallès are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, the extensive network of paths within Collserola Natural Park, and the opportunity to explore both challenging singletracks and more relaxed gravel roads amidst beautiful natural surroundings.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, beginners have several options. There are 7 easy mountain bike trails in the area. The Pla de Reixac, for instance, is known for its gentle slopes and numerous easy paths, making it a great spot for those new to mountain biking or looking for a relaxed ride. These trails allow you to enjoy the natural beauty of Collserola without overly demanding technical sections.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that are accessible by public transport or have parking nearby?
A: Sant Cugat Del Vallès is well-connected, and many trails are designed with accessibility in mind. Some routes, like 'Sant Cugat – Baixador de Vallvidrera' and 'Valldoreix – La Floresta', are specifically planned to end conveniently near train stations, making public transport a viable option. For those driving, various trailheads within the Collserola Natural Park and around Sant Cugat offer parking facilities, allowing easy access to the routes.

Q: Are there any specific trails known for unique riding experiences?
A: Yes, the region offers some distinctive trails. The Torrent de la Salamandra Singletrack is often highlighted for its unique character, described as a fun, often wet, and green trail with dense vegetation, providing a riding experience reminiscent of other lush, forested regions. This trail offers a different feel compared to the more open dirt and gravel roads found elsewhere in Collserola.
tips: This is a lovely horse farm next to the trail. The views of the pastures are beautiful.
An interesting climbing area. There are multiple short climbing routes here. If you are not a climber, it is still interesting to visit it on a walk or by bike.

A small pleasant waterfall that is unique within the Natural Park of Collserola, in the municipality of Molins de Rei. It is in a protected area for its great ecological value. It is a popular hiking and cycling destination from Molins de Rei or Sant Cugat.

Castellciuró is a castle located in the municipality of Molins de Rey, in Bajo Llobregat, in the Collserola mountain range, very close to the hermitage of Santa Cruz de Olorde and Puig de Olorda. It is currently in ruins, although these have been restored, and it also serves as a viewpoint.

   Documented in 1066, the oldest part of the remains of this castle is a wall in opus spicatum (work of inclined stones, alternating the direction of the inclination in each course) very well built, which could be of Visigothic origin (8th century). or pre-Romanesque (10th century) and were destroyed around the year 985 by the Saracen hordes of Almanzor. Of its scarce remains, a couple of walls and a cistern embedded in the rock stand out.

The Mirador del Portell de Valldaura is one of several viewpoints over the Serra de Collserola Natural Park. It is very wide and through it you can access the S-2 Portell de Valldaura MTB trail:

Portell de Valldaura on Trailforks
https://www.trailforks.com/trails/portell-de-valldaura/
One of several bridges along the Paseo de las Aguas. This crosses over the Carretera del Cementerio de Collserola.

This viewpoint gets its name from the peculiar star shape of its structure. From this point we are offered beautiful panoramic views of the Turó de Montcada and the Serralada de Marina.

    The vegetation in the area is made up of Mediterranean shrubs that combine with meadows, bushes and groves of Aleppo pine and oaks.
Where Collserola connects with Cerdanyola is the park called Canaletes. Here there are fields, forest and the Canaletes aqueduct.

There are plenty of great trails around Can Coll. It's a great place to stop for a hike, road bike ride, or mountain bike ride. You can even arrive by car to start an adventure. On one side of the road you have the historic complex of Can Coll and on the other you have a restaurant and public barbecues with water and a bar.

waypoints.name: Bridge over Cemetery Road in Collserola
